title: The Huntsmen
content:
Three jolly gentlemen,
        In coats of red,
    Rode their horses
        Up to bed.

    Three jolly gentlemen
        Snored till morn,
    Their horses champing
        The golden corn.

    Three jolly gentlemen,
        At break of day,
    Came clitter-clatter down the stairs
    And galloped away.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "The Huntsmen", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
